title: Ensure a log monitoring and alerts are set up for usage of "root"  account

input_row.title: Ensure a log monitoring and alerts are set up for usage of "root"  account
input_row.description: Real-time monitoring of API calls can be achieved by directing ActionTrail Logs to Log Service and establishing corresponding query and alarms. It is recommended that a query and alarm be established for console logins that are not protected by root login attempts.
input_row.rationale: Monitoring for root account logins will provide visibility into the use of a fully privileged account and an opportunity to reduce the use of it.
input_row.audit: Perform the following steps to ensure log monitoring and alerts are set for usage of “root” account. 1. Logon to SLS Console. 2. Click Log Service Audit Service in the navigation pane to go to the Log Service Audit Service page. 3. Ensure the Action Trail are Enabled under the Access to Cloud Products > Global Configuration page, and click Central Project. 4. Select Alerts. 5. Ensure below alert rule has been enabled and saved in the target actiontrail_log "event.eventName": ConsoleSignin and "event.userIdentity.type" : root-account
input_row.remediation: Perform the following to ensure the log monitoring and alerts are set up for usage of “root” account: 1. Logon to SLS Console. 2. Click Log Service Audit Service in the navigation pane. 3. Go to Access to Cloud Products > Global Configuration page. a. Select a location of project for logs. b. Check the Action Trail and configure a proper days. c. Click Save to save the changes. 89 | P a g e 4. Go to Access to Cloud Products > Global Configurations click Central Project. 5. Select Log Management > Actiontrail Log. 6. In the search/analytics console, input below query "event.eventName": ConsoleSignin and "event.userIdentity.type" : root-account 7. Create a dashboard and set alert for the query result.
input_row.default_value: The monitoring dashboard and alert is not set by default.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Manual

**MANUAL STEPS:**
1. Log in to the Alibaba Cloud Console.
2. In the navigation pane, click on "Log Service" and then select "Log Service Audit Service".
3. Navigate to "Access to Cloud Products" and select "Global Configuration". 
4. Verify that the "Action Trail" is enabled.
   - **What to verify/validate:** Ensure that the Action Trail is active and configured to collect logs.
   - **Expected secure configuration:** Action Trail should be enabled.
5. Click on "Central Project" under "Global Configuration" and select "Log Management" followed by "Actiontrail Log".
6. In the search/analytics console, input the following query: 
   - `"event.eventName": ConsoleSignin` and `"event.userIdentity.type": root-account`
   - **What to verify/validate:** Confirm that the query returns logs when the root account is used to sign in.
   - **Expected secure configuration:** The query should yield results that show usage of the root account.
7. To set alerts, select a dashboard in the Log Service and create an alert based on the query result.
   - **What to verify/validate:** Ensure alerts are configured correctly to notify when the specific criteria are met.
   - **Expected secure configuration:** Alerts should be set for any sign-ins by the root account.

By following these steps, you'll ensure that log monitoring and alerts for the usage of the root account are properly set up as per the CIS compliance control requirements.
